title: ⟐mniReality Node
description: Data shapes for nodes within the ⟐mniReality logic tree. Two storage keys exist per node — the core record (omni:nodes) owned by OmniNode, and the extended inspector record (omni:inspector:{id}) owned by OmniInspector. NodeLoader reads Shape 1. NodeLoader never touches Shape 2.

MediaItem.description: A single media attachment — file or URL

src.description: File path, data URI, or external URL

label.description: Display name — filename or URL tail

type.description: MIME type (present on file uploads, absent on URL entries)

OmniNodeExt.title: ⟐mniReality Node — Inspector Extension
OmniNodeExt.description: Extended per-node data owned by OmniInspector. Stored at localStorage key omni:inspector:{id}. Never parsed by NodeLoader.

text.description: Text content embedded on the node surface geometry

material.description: Three.js material type — selected in OmniInspector material dropdown

wireframe.description: Whether wireframe mode is active. Supported on MeshBasicMaterial and MeshStandardMaterial only — OmniInspector guards this.

scale.description: Per-axis scale controlled by OmniInspector scale inputs. Synced from mesh.scale on node load.

texture.description: URL or file path of applied texture image. Full base64 data URIs are stripped before save (too large for localStorage) — only URL strings persist.

images.description: Image attachments. Base64 data URI entries are stripped on save.

sound.description: Audio attachments. Base64 data URI entries are stripped on save.

media.description: General media attachments (video, documents, etc.). Base64 data URI entries are stripped on save.

type.description: MIME type — present on file uploads, absent on URL entries

description: Shape 1 — omni:nodes — core node record persisted by OmniNode

id.description: Auto-generated unique identifier. Format: omni_ + 6 base-36 random chars. Read-only after creation.

label.description: User-defined display name. Editable via OmniInspector. Used as display text in OmniNode list, OmniPresenter sequence, OmniPocket registry, NodeManager tree, and TreeView.

geometry.description: Three.js geometry type. Determines which GEOMETRY_DEFS factory is called when building the mesh. EdgesGeometry and WireframeGeometry render as THREE.LineSegments; all others render as THREE.Mesh.

primitive.description: Epistemological classification. Drives the default mesh color via PRIMITIVE_COLORS map in OmniNode. No ⟐ prefix — bare enum keys only.

_colorMap.undefined: #888888 — gray   — unknown, unresolved, not yet classified

color.description: Hex color string. Set from PRIMITIVE_COLORS on creation. Overridable per-node via OmniInspector color picker. Format: 6-digit lowercase hex with # prefix.

position.description: World position [x, y, z]. Stored as a 3-element number array. Consumed via array destructuring: const [nx, ny, nz] = node.position. Object form {x,y,z} is NOT used — the array form is canonical.

parentId.description: ID of the direct parent node. null if this node has no parent (root-level nodes). When set on creation, OmniNode draws a LineGeometry edge between parent and child. NodeManager reads this to build the _tree adjacency map.

rootId.description: ID of the origin root node of the tree this node belongs to. Read by OmniInspector for display in the hierarchy section. Not computed dynamically — must be set at creation time or by NodeLoader. null for root nodes (they are their own root).

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of node creation. Set once at creation via new Date().toISOString(). Never updated.

_storageNotes.omni:nodes: JSON array of Shape 1 objects. Written and read by OmniNode.
_storageNotes.omni:edges: JSON array of { from: string, to: string } edge records. Written and read by OmniNode.
_storageNotes.omni:inspector:{id}: Shape 2 (OmniNodeExt) for node with that id. Written and read by OmniInspector only.
_storageNotes.omni:nodemanager: NodeManager internal state: { current, back[], forward[] }. Not a node record.
_storageNotes.splitStorageRationale: Shape 1 is the lightweight runtime identity a node always needs. Shape 2 is heavier inspector data loaded only when a node is selected. Keeping them separate avoids bloating omni:nodes with texture/media data.
